subject: History
title: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s-1947

questions.text: The Indian National Congress (INC) was established in which year?
questions.explanation: INC was founded in December 1885 at Bombay.

questions.text: Who was the first President of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Womesh Chandra Bonnerji presided over the first session.

questions.text: Who is known as the 'Grand Old Man of India'?
questions.explanation: Naoroji was a businessman and publicist who settled in London.

questions.text: Who gave the slogan 'Freedom is my birthright and I shall have it'?
questions.explanation: Tilak was a leader of the Radicals in the INC.

questions.text: The Partition of Bengal took place in ________ during the Viceroyalty of Lord Curzon.
questions.explanation: The British divided Bengal ostensibly for administrative convenience.

questions.text: The movement against the Partition of Bengal was known as the:
questions.explanation: It emphasized self-help, national education, and use of Indian languages.

questions.text: The Muslim League was formed in which city in 1906?
questions.explanation: A group of Muslim landlords and nawabs formed the All India Muslim League.

questions.text: The split between Moderates and Radicals occurred in the ________ session of 1907.
questions.explanation: The two groups reunited in 1915.

questions.text: Gandhiji arrived in India from South Africa in ________.
questions.explanation: He had already earned international respect for his satyagraha struggles.

questions.text: The Rowlatt Act was passed in ________, giving the government powers to imprison people without trial.
questions.explanation: Gandhiji described it as 'devilish' and tyrannical.

questions.text: The Jallianwala Bagh massacre occurred on ________ 1919 in Amritsar.
questions.explanation: General Dyer ordered fire on a peaceful crowd celebrating Baisakhi.

questions.text: Who renounced his Knighthood in protest against the Jallianwala Bagh massacre?
questions.explanation: Tagore expressed the pain and anger of the country through this act.

questions.text: The Non-Cooperation movement was launched in ________.
questions.explanation: It involved boycotting British schools, courts, and foreign cloth.

questions.text: Gandhiji called off the Non-Cooperation movement due to violence at ________ in 1922.
questions.explanation: A mob of peasants set fire to a police station, killing 22 policemen.

questions.text: Who was the founder of the Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS)?
questions.explanation: RSS was formed in 1925 in Nagpur.

questions.text: The Dandi March (Salt Satyagraha) began in which year?
questions.explanation: Gandhiji walked 240 miles to break the salt law.

questions.text: The Government of India Act of ________ introduced provincial autonomy.
questions.explanation: The Congress won elections in 7 out of 11 provinces under this Act.

questions.text: The Quit India movement was launched in ________.
questions.explanation: Gandhiji gave the call 'Do or Die' (Karo ya Maro).

questions.text: Who formed the Indian National Army (INA)?
questions.explanation: Also known as the Azad Hind Fauj, it aimed to free India with Japanese help.

questions.text: In 1940, the Muslim League passed a resolution demanding ________ for Muslims.
questions.explanation: It demanded 'Independent States' in the North-Western and Eastern areas of India.

questions.text: The 'Direct Action Day' was announced by the Muslim League on ________ 1946.
questions.explanation: This led to massive riots in Calcutta and other parts of India.

questions.text: Who was the first woman President of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Annie Besant presided over the 1917 session.

questions.text: The Khilafat movement was organized by:
questions.explanation: Mohammad Ali and Shaukat Ali led the movement to protest against the treatment of the Turkish Caliph.

questions.text: The Simon Commission was boycotted because:
questions.explanation: All its members were British, which was seen as an insult to Indians.

questions.text: The 'Purna Swaraj' (Complete Independence) resolution was passed at the ________ session in 1929.
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru was the President of this historic session.

questions.text: Bhagat Singh and B.K. Dutt threw a bomb in the ________ in 1929.
questions.explanation: They did it to protest against anti-labor laws and the Rowlatt Act.

questions.text: Who was the Viceroy of India when the Quit India movement started?
questions.explanation: Linlithgow oversaw the suppression of the movement.

questions.text: The Cabinet Mission arrived in India in ________.
questions.explanation: It suggested a loose confederation for a united India, which was rejected.

questions.text: Who was the 'Frontier Gandhi'?
questions.explanation: He was a Pashtun leader and founder of the Khudai Khidmatgars.

questions.text: Lord Mountbatten's Plan for Partition was accepted in ________ 1947.
questions.explanation: The June 3rd Plan outlined the partition of India into two nations.

questions.text: The term 'Satyagraha' means:
questions.explanation: It was Gandhiji's method of non-violent protest based on truth.

questions.text: The 'Lucknow Pact' was signed in 1916 between INC and ________.
questions.explanation: It marked a temporary unity between the two major political groups.

questions.text: The Ilbert Bill (1883) caused a controversy because it sought to allow ________.
options: Indian judges to try British subjects
questions.explanation: The British opposition to this bill angered Indians and helped unify national sentiment.

questions.text: A publicist is someone who ________.
questions.explanation: The term was used for leaders like Dadabhai Naoroji who published pamphlets.

questions.text: The Vernacular Press Act was passed in ________ to silence critics of the government.
questions.explanation: It allowed the government to confiscate assets of newspapers that published 'seditious' material.

questions.text: Who was the 'moderate' leader who criticized the 'Radicals' for being impulsive?
questions.explanation: Moderates believed in constitutional methods and petitions.

questions.text: The Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA) was founded in ________ in 1928.
questions.explanation: It was led by Chandra Shekhar Azad and Bhagat Singh.

questions.text: C. Rajagopalachari led a salt march in which province?
questions.explanation: He led the Vedaranyam Salt Satyagraha.

questions.text: Who was the British Prime Minister during India's independence?
questions.explanation: Attlee was the leader of the Labor Party.

questions.text: The first independence day of India was celebrated on January 26, ________ as per the Lahore resolution.
questions.explanation: This date was later chosen for Republic Day.
